\newcommand{\JsonifiableFailureExampleRows}{%
prop-target-not-supported & 154 & Corelib.Classes.CMorphisms.apply\_subrelation & Targets live in Prop, while Jsonifiable builds computational data in Type. \\
to-json-generation-not-supported & 35 & Corelib.Init.Specif.sig & Generation rejected the declaration; these are treated as current limitations requiring inspection. \\
record-generation-not-supported & 32 & Corelib.ssr.ssrbool.predType & The record contains fields without an ordinary data representation, such as proofs, functions, Type fields, or dependent fields. \\
scanner-or-logical-path & 18 & Stdlib.FSets.FMapAVL.Raw.tree & The benchmark scanner found a source declaration whose guessed logical path is not directly derivable. \\
to-json-elaboration-failed & 7 & Corelib.ssr.ssrbool.if\_spec & Rocq rejected the generated term; these are possible deriver gaps or unsupported dependent shapes. \\
indexed-family-not-supported & 3 & Corelib.Init.Datatypes.reflect & The declaration is a family indexed by values, not a plain data type after parameters. \\
coinductive-not-supported & 1 & Stdlib.Streams.Streams.Stream & The declaration is potentially infinite data, while the deriver targets inductive data. \\
universe-polymorphism-not-supported & 1 & Stdlib.Logic.StrictProp.sEmpty & The declaration uses universe behavior outside the current monomorphic deriver path. \\
}
